p.seg: To All sons of Holy Mother Church present and future, Eustace de Vescy sends greeting.

Let present and future know me to have granted and by this my present charter to have made firm to God and to St Mary at Melrose and the monks serving God in that place all the alms and lands that they had and held in my feu in the year immediately before Alexander son of the lord William, king of the Scots, made his homage to John the king of England on the morrow of the Invention of the Holy Cross; in free, pure, and perpetual alms, that is, the whole of what they had in the territory of Whitton, both from the donation of Patrick of Ryedale and from the gift of Robert of Barnoldby, and from the gift of Geoffrey son of Waldef of Lilliesleaf, as it is contained in their charters and their authentic [deeds] bear witness. And the whole alms that they had in the territory of Mow from the gift of Anselm of Whitton, as his charters bear witness. And the whole land which they had in the territory of Primside by the agreement made by the king of Scotland between the monastic house of Melrose and the monastic house of Kelso as the confirmation of the lord king in respect of the same land bears witness. Wherefore I will and enjoin that the foresaid monks of Melrose have, hold, and possess all the forementioned lands and alms in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures and peateries, in moors and marshes, as freely, fully, quitely, peaceably, and honourably as they quitely, fully, and honourably hold and possess any alms in the kingdom of Scotland.
